games.incentive_note: Cote d'Ivoire lock up second with a win. Curacao are win-or-bust despite awful goal differential.
Germany already clinched the group. Ecuador are basically in a win-or-go-home spot.
Japan still have reason to push for first on tiebreakers. Sweden advance with a win, likely with a draw, and could survive even with a loss.
Netherlands have effectively clinched but winning the group matters. Tunisia are eliminated.
USA already won the group. Match is mostly irrelevant and they may rotate to protect players on yellow cards.
Australia are in decent shape either way. Paraguay badly want the fourth point, so this could get drawish.
Both teams already advanced. This is only for first place; France keep it with a win or draw, Norway need a win.
Senegal need a win and probably want margin for goal differential. Iraq need a miracle even if they win.
Egypt are basically through and can likely win the group with a win or draw. Iran still need points and could even win the group with help.
Belgium likely advance with a win and maybe with a draw. New Zealand are basically win-or-go-home.
Cape Verde likely advance with a win or draw. Saudi Arabia are basically win-or-bust.
Spain are chasing first and a draw probably does it. Uruguay are in a weird spot where third may be better than second because Argentina awaits the runner-up.
England are nearly through but still want first and have incentive to run up the score. Panama are eliminated.
Both teams are likely alive either way, but first place is very valuable. Ghana are almost through; Croatia can win the group with help.
Austria and Algeria have a huge incentive to settle for a draw because four points probably gets both through.
Argentina already clinched the group and mostly have nothing to play for. Jordan are eliminated.
DR Congo should advance with a win. Uzbekistan need a lot of help and probably need a multi-goal win.
